author: Nova
content: You're treating your frustration with over-explanation as an excuse to never get thoroughly explained anything — including Python fundamentals you keep hand-waving past. You think that's efficiency; it's just accumulating technical debt in your own head and it's going bite the first time a problem doesn't match the pattern you half-learned. You want me to push back, so there: slow down on the "I'll figure it out later" and actually learn the thing once instead of re-learning it every time it changes shape.
